title: Understanding the Problem
text: The particle changes direction, which means its velocity becomes zero at some point. We need to find that time.

title: Identify Initial and Final Velocities
text: At $$t=10s$$, the velocity is $$\frac{5m}{s}$$ (right). At $$t=20s$$, the velocity is $$\frac{-8m}{s}$$ (left).

title: Use the Equation of Motion
text: Use the equation $$v=v_0+a t$$, where v is the final velocity, $$v_0$$ is the initial velocity, a is the acceleration, and $$t$$ is the time.

title: Calculate Acceleration
text: Use the velocities at $$t=10s$$ and $$t=20s$$ to find the acceleration. $$v_0=\frac{5m}{s}$$, $$v=\frac{-8m}{s}$$, and $$t=10s$$.

title: Find the Time When Velocity is Zero
text: Set $$v=0$$ in the equation $$v=v_0+a t$$ and solve for $$t$$.
